proofs.proof_tex:
Fix p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}. By def:feasible-designs, epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon for every k in I_n. Thus the positivity bounds used below come from feasible-set membership, not from a separate positivity assumption. For each i in O_n, ass:bounded-outcome-degree gives d_i = |N_i(G_n)| <= dbar. Since epsilon in (0,1), pi_i^1(p) = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} p_k >= epsilon^{d_i} >= epsilon^{dbar}, and pi_i^0(p) = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} (1-p_k) >= epsilon^{d_i} >= epsilon^{dbar}.

Put U_i = T_i(Z)/pi_i^1(p) - 1 and V_i = C_i(Z)/pi_i^0(p) - 1. By ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, E_p T_i(Z) = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} p_k = pi_i^1(p), and E_p C_i(Z) = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} (1-p_k) = pi_i^0(p). Therefore E_p U_i = E_p V_i = 0. By def:first-order-linearization, eta_i(p,Z) = U_i (Y_i^1-mu_1) - V_i (Y_i^0-mu_0). The centered potential-outcome terms are fixed finite-population constants, so E_p eta_i(p,Z) = 0.

By lem:centered-potential-outcome-bound, |Y_i^1-mu_1| <= 2 and |Y_i^0-mu_0| <= 2. Also T_i(Z)/pi_i^1(p) takes values in {0,1/pi_i^1(p)}, so |U_i| <= max{1,1/pi_i^1(p)-1} <= 1/pi_i^1(p) <= epsilon^{-dbar}; similarly |V_i| <= epsilon^{-dbar}. Hence |eta_i(p,Z)| <= 2 epsilon^{-dbar} + 2 epsilon^{-dbar} = 4 epsilon^{-dbar} for every i.

Next, eta_i(p,Z) is a measurable function only of the assignment coordinates {Z_k : k in N_i(G_n)}. If S_{ij}(G_n) = emptyset, then N_i(G_n) and N_j(G_n) are disjoint. Ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli makes the corresponding assignment subvectors independent, and therefore eta_i(p,Z) and eta_j(p,Z) are independent. Thus the graph on O_n with edges between distinct i,j satisfying S_{ij}(G_n) != emptyset is a dependency graph for {eta_i(p,Z) : i in O_n}. Its degree at vertex i is at most |{j in O_n : S_{ij}(G_n) != emptyset}|, which is bounded by Delta_n; ass:bounded-overlap-dependency gives Delta_n <= Dbar.

Finally, since the eta_i(p,Z) are centered, Var_p(n^{-1/2} sum_{i in O_n} eta_i(p,Z)) = n^{-1} sum_{i,j in O_n} E_p[eta_i(p,Z) eta_j(p,Z)]. By def:variance-scale, the right-hand side is exactly sigma_{G_n,p}^2(Y). This proves the centering, the uniform 4 epsilon^{-dbar} bound, the dependency-graph claim with maximum degree at most Dbar, and the stated variance identity.

added_lemmas.statement: Under ass:bounded-outcomes, for every i in O_n, |Y_i^1-mu_1| <= 2 and |Y_i^0-mu_0| <= 2.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By ass:bounded-outcomes, |Y_j^1| <= 1 for every j in O_n. Since mu_1 = n^{-1} sum_{j in O_n} Y_j^1, the triangle inequality gives |mu_1| <= n^{-1} sum_{j in O_n} |Y_j^1| <= 1. Hence |Y_i^1-mu_1| <= |Y_i^1| + |mu_1| <= 2 for every i. The same argument with the all-control schedule gives |mu_0| <= 1 and therefore |Y_i^0-mu_0| <= |Y_i^0| + |mu_0| <= 2.
